name: output-format-enforcer
prompt:
Add an output format enforcer for the execute phase.

In agentrail/run/output_enforcer.py expose:
- Accepted and Rejected result types (a Rejected carries a non-empty reason string explaining the rejection)
- enforce(content, is_new_or_rename=False) -> Accepted | Rejected
- all_changes_new_or_rename(porcelain_str) -> bool (given `git status --porcelain` text, are all changed paths new or renames?)
- push_format_rejection_event(workdir, run_id, kind, reason) -> bool (non-fatal; emits a session event)

Intent: when the agent edits an existing file it must return a unified diff, not a full-file rewrite. enforce treats the presence of a unified-diff hunk header ('@@ ... @@') anywhere in the content as a valid diff and accepts it; with is_new_or_rename=True it accepts any content including empty; otherwise (editing an existing file) content with no hunk header — empty content included — is Rejected, and the Rejected reason references the expected '@@' hunk-header marker. all_changes_new_or_rename returns True only when every git-porcelain entry is an add, untracked, or rename and there is at least one entry; a modified existing file, a mix that includes one, or empty/whitespace input returns False. push_format_rejection_event records a rejection as a session event whose session_id is run_id, whose top-level kind equals the kind argument, and whose action has type 'output_format_rejected', phase equal to kind, and the reason; it returns True when the event was accepted (HTTP 202) and False when the workdir is not linked to a server or the push errors, and it must never raise.
